titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Anna Marble-Pollock Cat Collection
titlestmt.author: by Brooke McManus

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday June 28, 2023 at 03:24

did.unittitle: Anna Marble-Pollock cat collection

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.physdesc: (27 boxes) + 15 art, 2 roll, 1 broadside, 1 record album storage
did.abstract: The collection contains photographs and other visual materials, objects, papers, printed material, and sound recordings relating to cats, Anna Marble-Pollock, and Carl Van Vechten.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 30 (sound recordings): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Received from various sources, and gift of Carl Van Vechten.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: I. Photographs, 1880s-1960. II. Visual Materials, 1827-1964, undated. III. Objects and Papers, 1908-circa 1966, undated. IV. Index Cards and Printed Material, 1949-1950s, undated. V. Sound Recordings, circa 1922-1950s.

bioghist.head: Anna Marble-Pollock (1881-1946)
bioghist.p: Anna Marble-Pollock was a writer and theatrical press agent. Born in Chicago in 1881, she began a career as a newspaper reporter in New York City in 1899. Marble-Pollock also authored short stories and the vaudeville sketch In Old Edam . In the early 1900s, she began a career as a publicity agent for actors, producers, theaters, and playhouses. Among her clients were Elsie de Wolfe and Oscar Hammerstein. Marble-Pollock also bred cats. She died in 1946. After her death, her friend Carl Van Vechten founded the Anna Marble Pollock Memorial Library of Books about Cats at Yale University.

bioghist.head: Carl Van Vechten (1880-1964)
bioghist.p: Carl Van Vechten was a writer, photographer, collector, and patron of the arts. Born in Cedar Rapids, Iowa, on June 17, 1880, Van Vechten developed an early interest in literature and the fine and performing arts. He attended the University of Chicago, and wrote for several Chicago newspapers before moving to New York City in 1906. Over the next five decades, Van Vechten established himself as a journalist, critic, novelist, and photographer. He published The Tiger in the House , a treatise on cats, in 1920. He was also a collector of cat-themed artwork. In 1947, Van Vechten established the Anna Marble Pollock Collection of Books about Cats at Yale University.
Van Vechten married Fania Marinoff, an actor who had immigrated to the United States as a child from Russia, in 1914. They were married for fifty years.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Anna Marble-Pollock Cat Collection. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
Box 8, a repository created container, was discarded, creating a gap in the box number sequence.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains photographs and other visual materials, objects, papers, printed material, and sound recordings relating to cats, Anna Marble-Pollock, and Carl Van Vechten.

Photographs depict cats at home and outdoors, in art, and with human companions, including Marble-Pollock, Van Vechten, Leonor Fini, and Anna May Wong. Photographers include Van Vechten and Sanford Roth. Visual materials consist of pen and wash and pencil drawings, watercolor paintings, lithographs, etchings, engravings, color prints from woodblocks, printed ephemera, playing cards, postage stamps, a kakemono, and other works depicting cats. Among the artists present in the collection are Leonor Fini, Francisco Goya, Hiroshige, Utagawa Kuniyoshi, Édouard Manet, Théophile Alexandre Steinman, and Florine Stettheimer. Objects and papers contain ceramic and porcelain cat figurines collected by Van Vechten; a ceramic sugar bowl; a brass door knocker; a wool blanket, neckties, and handkerchiefs; slate and Mercer tiles; a sterling silver bottle stopper; gold and silver brooches; calendars and greeting cards; and various cat-themed decorative items. Also included are a cat scratching post invented in the 1950s and cat show prize ribbons from 1908 and 1909. Other materials in the collection include index cards with bibliographic information and notes on works about cats, possibly used by Van Vechten for research; a set of miniature children's books by Dorothy Kunhardt titled Tiny Nonsense Stories ; and three phonograph records with readings and music relating to cats.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Anna Marble-Pollock Cat Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: Photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains photographic prints, cabinet cards, and photographic postcards of cats by Carl Van Vechten and others. Photographs show artist and author Leonor Fini, actor Anna May Wong, writer W. L. George, and Anna Marble-Pollock posing with cats. Some images in this series were included in Van Vechten's book The Tiger in the House . Also included are photographs of artwork featuring cats and Van Vechten with two of his cat figurines, "Helen" and "Oregano."

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by folder title, with miscellaneous photographs filed at the end of the sequence.

did.unittitle: Pollock-Marble, Anna and her cats
Van Vechten, Carl
William Rockhill Nelson Gallery of Art
Miscellaneous

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a childhood portrait of Anna Marble-Pollock in the 1880s.

did.unittitle: Visual Materials

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: All materials were the gift of Carl Van Vechten unless indicated otherwise.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Not arranged in any particular order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of drawings, paintings, lithographs, engravings, etchings, posters, three decks of playing cards, gift wrapping paper and postage stamps, a few photographs, a kakemono, and other visual materials relating to cats. Included are original works or reproductions by artists, photographers, and printers such as Peggy Bacon, Adolfo Best-Maugard, Currier & Ives, Francisco Goya, Hiroshige, Paul Klee, Utagawa Kuniyoshi, Leonor Fini, Luigi Lucioni, Édouard Manet, Sanford Roth, Théophile Alexandre Steinlen, Florine Stettheimer, and Prentiss Taylor.

did.unittitle: Best-Maugard, Adolfo. "Tallulah," oil on board, signed and framed

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Fania Marinoff Van Vechten, 1970.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drawing for illustration on page 62 of Chapter IV of Cats in our Lives , by James Mason and Pamela Kellino. Includes publisher's letter.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition

scopecontent.p: Color illustration of a Manx cat standing on a map of the Isle of Man.

did.unittitle: Objects and Papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of hardware, jewelry, textiles, artwork, kitchen and household accessories, ephemera, and other materials featuring representations of cats. Included are a brass door knocker; ceramic sugar bowl; sterling silver bottle stopper; wooden letter holder; decorative tiles; ceramic, porcelain, and wooden cat figurines; gold and silver brooches; stationery and handmade greeting cards; a wool blanket; and several neckties and handkerchiefs. The series also contains a cat scratching post and cat show prize ribbons.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged roughly by format or object type.

did.unittitle: Calendar with black velvet cat

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Message to "Boodle Boo" on verso.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brooch given to Kathryn Wilson, Anna Marble-Pollock's mother, by Polish actor Helena Modjeska. Engraved "Kitten for Kittie Modjeska." Originally housed in Saks Fifth Avenue ring box.

White cat with black striped head, seated (porcelain)
Index Cards and Printed Material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes index cards containing bibliographic and research information on cats and cat-related topics, as well as Tiny Nonsense Stories , a series of miniature children's books by Dorothy Kunhardt. The series also contains materials related to two 10" phonograph records, consisting of an original box and ephemera for a reading of T. S. Eliot poems entitled "Old Possum's Book of Practical Cats" and a record cover for "Look at the Kitty Cat," sung by Burl Ives.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Index cards A-Z
Index cards - Poetry related to cats, A-W
Index cards - Fiction, folklore, and poetry related to cats, A-Z
Index cards - Notes related to cats and works about cats
Kunhardt, Dorothy, and Garth Williams. Tiny Nonsense Stories : 12 Story Books
Record covers and ephemera related to phonograph records

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also: Series V. Sound Recordings, Box 30.

did.unittitle: Sound Recordings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of three 10" phonograph records containing readings and music related to cats, performed by Robert Donat, Burl Ives, and Zez Confrey and His Orchestra.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Eliot, T. S., "Old Possum's Book of Practical Cats," six poems by T. S. Eliot, read by Robert Donat

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also: Series IV. Index Cards and Printed Material, Box 29.

did.unittitle: Ives, Burl [sung by], "Look at the Little Kitty Kat" and "The Whistling Rabbit," Decca record

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also: Series IV. Index Cards and Printed Material, Box 29.

did.unittitle: Zez Confrey and His Orchestra, "Kitten on the Keys - Foxtrot"
